headline: Human Trafficking Cases Rise 28% Keeping Tier 2 Status
context: Malaysia recorded 580 human trafficking cases in 2025 a 28% increase remaining on US Tier 2 Watch List.

cards.big: Trafficking cases rose 28% to 580 keeping Malaysia on Tier 2 Watch List
cards.sub: Victims primarily from Indonesia Bangladesh and Myanmar exploited in plantations

cards.big: Only 45 trafficking convictions from 580 identified cases in 2025
cards.sub: Conviction rate of 7.8% reflects challenges in testimony and evidence

cards.big: 86 officers across 3 agencies investigated for complicity in networks
cards.sub: MACC arrested 12 immigration officers for facilitating victim entry

cards.big: Government spent RM28 million on 8 shelters serving 1,200 victims
cards.sub: Average shelter stay of 14 months restricts victim mobility and recovery

cards.big: Trafficking response tests whether enforcement targets perpetrators or just processes victims
28% rise in trafficking cases keeps Malaysia at Tier 2. Enforcement alone cannot fix systemic labour exploitation.
